_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Decreased incidence of epilepsy after cranioplasty
Avoidance of recurrence of brain damage
Relief of syndrome of trephine (i.e., headaches, dizziness, intolerance of vibration and noise, irritability, fatigability, loss of motivation and concentration, depression, and anxiety)
Promotion of resumption of brain tissue
Treatment of encephalocele skull defects with neurological cognition and mental syndrome

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indication of procedure, and planned line of treatment

claim.label: Post Procedure X-ray with report of skull

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure, and planned line of treatment?
Was CT/MRI skull report of patient submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Were the detailed indoor case papers submitted?
Was post procedure clinical photograph submitted?
Was the post procedure X-ray report of skull submitted?
Are the detailed Procedure/ Operative notes submitted?
Is the invoice/barcode of implant submitted?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes detailing history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure, and planned line of treatment not submitted at pre-authorization.
CT/MRI skull report of patient not submitted at pre-authorization.
Detailed indoor case papers not submitted at claim submission.
Post-procedure X-ray report of skull not submitted at claim submission.
Detailed Procedure/Operative notes not submitted at claim submission.
Invoice/Barcode details of implant not submitted at claim submission.
Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up not submitted at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Cranioplasty, the repair of a skull vault defect by insertion of an object (bone or nonbiological materials such as metal or plastic plates), is a well-known procedure in modern neurosurgery.

It is important not only for cosmetic and protection of underlying brain but also for restoring the dynamics of a closed cavity, which are disturbed when in the absence of overlying bone the atmospheric pressure is allowed to exert an influence.
